topic.what: MRG (Map Reduce Graph) - unsupervised method for learning HTTP REST API structure from traffic and generating OpenAPI specs
topic.how: Combines tree-based decomposition, graph modeling, and deep autoencoder for structural and semantic anomaly detection without labeled data
topic.why_matters: Addresses API security blind spots (84% of orgs had incidents, $94-186B annual losses) by automating schema inference and detecting structural/content anomalies in dynamic microservice environments

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of flagged requests that are truly malicious
characteristics.vs_baseline: Perfect precision on ATRDF2; 98.77% on CSIC 2010 (vs 95.38% FT-ANN)
characteristics.context: Perfect across all URL-embedded attacks (SQLi, XSS, RCE, etc.) on ATRDF2; 97.23% on ATRDF

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of malicious requests correctly detected
characteristics.vs_baseline: 93.51% macro avg on ATRDF (vs 82.07% HRAL, 100% FT-ANN); 69.78% on CSIC 2010
characteristics.context: Strong on ATRDF/ATRDF2 but struggles with CSIC 2010 synthetic payloads and obfuscated LOG4J attacks (54.59% recall)

characteristics.vs_baseline: ~20x faster than FT-ANN; 10x faster than HRAL on ATRDF
characteristics.context: 0.0001s fastest on ATRDF2, 0.00036s on CSIC 2010; low std dev indicates consistent performance

characteristics.what_it_measures: Legitimate requests incorrectly flagged as anomalies
characteristics.context: Perfect precision achieved across ATRDF2; some false negatives but no false positives on structured datasets

characteristics.vs_baseline: First unsupervised method to generate OpenAPI-compliant documentation without prior knowledge
characteristics.context: Enables real-time documentation updates and shadow API detection

characteristics.vs_baseline: Does not require labeled data unlike FT-ANN, HRAL baselines
characteristics.context: Fully unsupervised learning from raw traffic; eliminates labeling bottleneck common in security applications
